summary: Verified BranchContext.js fix - fetchPendingOrdersCounts initialization error resolved. All critical flows working: Login, Dashboard, POS page load without white screen.

integration_issues.issue: Error accessing index 'is_synced' in store 'orders' - DataError when calling getItemsByIndex
integration_issues.details: Known issue from previous iterations. Non-critical - doesn't break app functionality.

critical_code_review_comments: BranchContext.js fix verified - fetchPendingOrdersCounts is now defined with useCallback before being used in fetchBranches
context_for_next_testing_agent: BranchContext.js fix verified. The fetchPendingOrdersCounts function is now properly defined with useCallback at line 51 before being used in fetchBranches. No white screen issues. IndexedDB is_synced error is a known non-critical issue.

tests_performed.details: Login page loads correctly with email/password form
Login with hanialdujaili@gmail.com / Hani@2024 works correctly. User has admin role.
Dashboard loads without errors. Shows statistics: 5K IQD sales, 1 order, 5,000 IQD average, 3K IQD profit
POS page loads with content - categories (4), products with prices visible. No white screen issue.
Branches loaded and saved to localStorage (2 branches: Aljadraia, Alsaydaia)
Returns correct data - 2 branches with all fields
No 'Cannot access fetchPendingOrdersCounts before initialization' error. Function properly defined with useCallback before use.

console_errors_noted: Error accessing index 'is_synced' in store 'orders': DataError - known issue, non-critical
Some 403 errors during navigation - related to session/token refresh, non-critical
rca_of_the_issue: The original error 'Cannot access fetchPendingOrdersCounts before initialization' was caused by using the function before it was defined. The fix moved the useCallback definition of fetchPendingOrdersCounts to line 51, before it's used in fetchBranches at line 156. This follows JavaScript's temporal dead zone rules for const/let declarations.
